senses.definition: used to imply that in certain situations, equality is not applied consistently or fairly, and that certain individuals or groups may receive better treatment or privileges than others

examples.x: The fact that some individuals were able to gain access to vaccines before others, despite being in a lower priority group, showed that some are more equal than others when it comes to healthcare resources.

senses.definition: used to imply that conflicts between powerful entities can have serious consequences for those who are caught in the middle or who are weaker and more vulnerable

senses.definition: used to stress the importance of timely delivery of justice, as any delay can render it ineffective and meaningless

examples.x: The family of the victim was frustrated by the slow progress of the trial, feeling that justice delayed is justice denied.

senses.definition: used to imply that people who behave immorally or wickedly may be rewarded or protected while those who behave honestly may face difficulties or adversity

examples.x: After years of studying and working hard, she was shocked to see her less-qualified coworker receive a promotion instead of her - as some say, the devil looks after his own.

senses.definition: used to suggest that one side has an overwhelming advantage or that the competition is unfairly skewed in favor of one party

examples.x: The billionaire's legal team has so much experience and resources that it's all Lombard Street to a China orange for the small law firm representing the plaintiff.
